title: Keep blue-green rollout under one global Supervisor
summary: Resolved, merged, installed, and verified at main@86c75cd0ac4d9d9ffb9ba0c1e90a5a6dd9917c21. Single-root-Supervisor rollout and rollback, staged release publication, rollback release identity, slot-local lifecycle isolation, and active-slot status resolution are complete. Targeted process-level tests and all required named checks passed.
goals: Candidate slot startup must not bind the root Supervisor rescue/control port.
A stale or preinstalled candidate Supervisor release must not change candidate lifecycle ownership.
A verified rollout must install the immutable release at root Controller Home and schedule detached activation after cutover.
Rollout failure must leave active authority and root Supervisor untouched.
Rollback and candidate cleanup must use slot-local lifecycle.
nonGoals: Do not redesign stable ingress routing.
Do not remove blue/green slot homes.
Do not kill the active global Supervisor to free a port.
Do not change unrelated restart coordinator behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Only one global Stable Supervisor process exists during candidate startup and cutover.
Candidate startup succeeds even when candidateHome contains an installed Supervisor release.
Failed candidate verification preserves active slot authority and active Supervisor PID.
Successful rollout reports root release installation and detached activation metadata.
Targeted blue/green, type, architecture, MCP compatibility, and Controller V8 checks pass.

tasks.title: Enforce single-Supervisor blue-green lifecycle
tasks.objective: Add an internal slot-local lifecycle mode to Controller service start/stop so blue-green candidate and rollback paths bypass installed Stable Supervisor artifacts. Remove candidate-home Supervisor installation. After candidate verification, install the immutable release only in root Controller Home; after successful cutover verification, schedule detached root Supervisor activation and return its metadata. Add regression coverage for preinstalled candidate release and no second global Supervisor. Validate and merge to main, then clean branch/worktree.
acceptanceCriteria: Candidate lifecycle bypasses Stable Supervisor branch.
Root release install occurs only after candidate verification.
Detached root activation is scheduled only after successful cutover verification.
Candidate cleanup and rollback do not stop or launch root Supervisor.
Existing isolated rollout/rollback tests pass and new single-Supervisor regression passes.
notes: Implemented and merged as main@86c75cd0ac4d9d9ffb9ba0c1e90a5a6dd9917c21. Production rollout/rollback now stays under one root Stable Supervisor, uses unpublished staged immutable releases, publishes only after candidate verification, preserves rollback release identity, propagates slot-local lifecycle isolation, resolves root status from the active slot home, and uses a typed durable candidate smoke Job. Validation passed: package:check:type, package:check:mcp-compatibility, package:check:runtime-architecture, package:check:controller-v8, plus 21 Stable Supervisor hardening tests and 4 isolated blue/green lifecycle tests. The process-level isolated test completed blue→green→blue with the root Supervisor PID unchanged and no second global Supervisor. The merged release is installed and live; Daemon/Gateway/Scheduler/Workers/Projection are healthy on commit 86c75cd0. Full repository tests still contain unrelated existing/environment-sensitive failures, and release readiness remains blocked only by the pre-existing personal-path open-source audit. A live production rollout mutation was not executed because the platform blocked the authorized command and the current static connector snapshot did not expose the dedicated controller_rollout callable, so no bypass was used.
